compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
An agent-executed epic cannot be represented in the store as certified/**signed**-closed when
one of its child stories carries an `[operator-attested]` acceptance criterion whose evidence
lives outside the codebase and outside the executing agent's access — even when every
engineering acceptance criterion across the whole epic is demonstrably complete and landed on
`main`. This is a process/tooling gap surfaced while executing epic `bf50-2131-271b-4fdc`
(graceless-hominine-jerboa); it is NOT a defect in any of that epic's code changes.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Decompose an epic into stories and execute it with an agent (claim → implement → land each
   child through the Gerrit gate).
2. Include among the children a story whose acceptance criteria contain an `[operator-attested]`
   item whose admissible evidence is inherently out-of-band — e.g. 5f39-cb52-9355-492f AC3:
   configured GitHub repo VARIABLES (`JIRA_URL`/`JIRA_USER`/`JIRA_PROJECT`,
   `ACLI_VERSION`/`ACLI_SHA256`), a configured SECRET (`JIRA_API_TOKEN`), a provisioned
   dedicated Jira test project, and the URL of a green `workflow_dispatch` run in which the live
   Jira test EXECUTED (not all-skip).
3. Have the agent complete and LAND every code change (all children `Verified +1` AND
   `LLM-Review +1`, merged to `main`).
4. Attempt to close that child, then the epic.

## Expected
With all engineering acceptance criteria met and landed, the epic — and every child — can be
closed with a completion-verifier **signature** (certified), such that the store durably
records the epic as fully certified when the work is in fact complete.

## Actual
- The child's completion verifier passes every CODE criterion but FAILS the single
  `[operator-attested]` criterion, because the agent has neither the access nor the
  authorization to produce the required out-of-band evidence (repo secrets/variables, a live
  Jira project, an observed green canary run), and no concrete attestation (run URL/id/timestamp)
  therefore exists to record.
- The child can only be **force-closed UNSIGNED**.
- The epic's own acceptance criterion requires "all child stories closed (**signed**)", so the
  epic's completion verifier fails on that criterion and the epic can only be **force-closed
  UNSIGNED** as well.
- Net: an epic whose entire engineering scope is complete and landed cannot be represented as
  certified/signed, purely because one child's evidence is out-of-band from the agent's reach.

## Evidence / Impact
- Concretely observed on epic `bf50-2131-271b-4fdc`: 19/19 stories implemented and merged to
  `main` (Gerrit changes 537–555 + 559); 18/19 children closed SIGNED (plan-review +
  completion-verifier); child `5f39-cb52-9355-492f` and the epic closed UNSIGNED via force-close.
- Full trail (per-story claims, RED/teeth evidence, landing, the force-close reasoning, and a
  dedicated PROBLEM RECORD entry) is in session log `25f1-5000-760d-4c2b`
  (plump-kitschy-meadowlark), linked to this bug.
- Impact: the "signed = fully certified" invariant cannot be earned for any epic that contains
  an operator-attested criterion the agent cannot attest; such epics are indistinguishable in
  the store (both unsigned) from epics whose OWN engineering work genuinely failed verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The problem is reproducible and characterized: an agent-executed epic with all
      engineering ACs complete + landed, plus one child bearing an out-of-band
      `[operator-attested]` criterion the agent cannot attest, results in unsigned closure of
      both the child and the epic.
- [ ] The gap is documented with the concrete instance (epic bf50, child 5f39) and its evidence
      (the specific out-of-band items required, and why the agent could not produce them).
- [ ] This bug links to the full session log capturing the run and the problem record.
compiled_state.title: Agent-executed epic cannot close SIGNED when a child has an operator-attested criterion the agent cannot attest
